```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(DEoptim)
library(lmomco)
library(DEoptim)
library(tidyr)
```






```{r}
# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

# Input and Output Directories
metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir    <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Create the Plots directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD REGIONAL MASTER DATA
# =====================================================================
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))

cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
```



```{r}
t2_val<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(Station_ID=="DE_00020")
k_seq<-t2_val$k_hours
t2_val<-t2_val$t2_pos
l1<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(Station_ID=="DE_00020")
l1<-l1$l1_pos
l2<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(Station_ID=="DE_00020")
l2<-l2$l2_pos
```


```{r}
plot(l1)+plot(l2)
```


```{r}
library(DEoptim)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Define the Ratio of Power Laws Function
# f(k) = c + W * [ (k + d1)^-a / (k + d2)^-b ]
ratio_power_model <- function(k, alpha, beta, d1, d2, W, cc) {
  num <- (k + d1)^-alpha
  den <- (k + d2)^-beta
  return(cc + W * (num / den))
}

# 2. Filter data for k >= 24
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are already aligned in your environment
df_tail <- data.frame(k = k_seq, t2 = t2_val) %>%
  filter(k >= 24)

k_train <- df_tail$k
t2_train <- df_tail$t2

# 3. Objective Function
obj_func_tail <- function(params) {
  a   <- params[1]
  b   <- params[2]
  d1  <- params[3]
  d2  <- params[4]
  W   <- params[5]
  cc  <- params[6]
  
  preds <- ratio_power_model(k_train, a, b, d1, d2, W, cc)
  
  # Minimize SSE
  return(sum((t2_train - preds)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# 4. Optimization for the Tail
# We narrow the search for 'c' around your tail values (0.4 - 0.5)
set.seed(123)
fit_tail <- DEoptim(fn = obj_func_tail, 
                    lower = c(0.1, 0.1, 0.0, 0.0, 0.01, 0.35), 
                    upper = c(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 0.50), 
                    control = DEoptim.control(itermax = 300, trace = 50))

# 5. Extraction
res_t <- fit_tail$optim$bestmem
a_t <- res_t[1]; b_t <- res_t[2]
d1_t <- res_t[3]; d2_t <- res_t[4]
W_t <- res_t[5]; c_t <- res_t[6]

# 6. Visualization
k_smooth <- seq(24, 240, length.out = 200)
df_line <- data.frame(k = k_smooth) %>%
  mutate(y = ratio_power_model(k, a_t, b_t, d1_t, d2_t, W_t, c_t))

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = df_tail, aes(x = k, y = t2), size = 3, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = df_line, aes(x = k, y = y), color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Tail Fit (k >= 24): Ratio of Power Laws",
       subtitle = paste("Alpha:", round(a_t, 2), "| Beta:", round(b_t, 2), "| Limit C:", round(c_t, 4)),
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)") 
```

```{r}
# 1. Use the BEST parameters from your previous fit_tail optimization
# (Ensure these variables are in your environment from the previous step)
a_opt  <- res_t[1]
b_opt  <- res_t[2]
d1_opt <- res_t[3]
d2_opt <- res_t[4]
W_opt  <- res_t[5]
c_opt  <- res_t[6]

# 2. Generate predictions for ALL k (1 to 240)
k_all_seq <- seq(1, 240, length.out = 500)
df_validation <- data.frame(k = k_all_seq) %>%
  mutate(predicted = ratio_power_model(k, a_opt, b_opt, d1_opt, d2_opt, W_opt, c_opt))

# 3. Create a combined data frame for the original points
# Assuming t2_val and k_seq are your original full vectors
df_original <- data.frame(k = k_seq, observed = t2_val) %>%
  mutate(region = ifelse(k >= 24, "Training (Tail)", "Validation (Head)"))

# 4. Plot the results
ggplot() +
  # The Full Prediction Line
  geom_line(data = df_validation, aes(x = k, y = predicted), 
            color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  # The Data Points (Colored by region)
  geom_point(data = df_original, aes(x = k, y = observed, color = region), size = 3) +
  # Vertical line at the training threshold
  geom_vline(xintercept = 24, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Training (Tail)" = "black", "Validation (Head)" = "orange")) +
  labs(title = "Model Validation: Tail-Optimized Parameters on All Data",
       subtitle = "Does the tail-fit predict the low-k hump?",
       x = "Threshold (k)", y = "L-CV (t2)",
       color = "Data Region") +
  theme_minimal()
```






```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(DEoptim)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# =========================================================================
# 1. Define the Stretched Surge Model (Kohlrausch)
# =========================================================================
stretched_surge_model <- function(k, c, A, alpha, beta, gamma) {
  return(c + A * (k^alpha) * exp(-(beta * k)^gamma))
}

# =========================================================================
# 2. Define the Objective Function
# =========================================================================
obj_stretched <- function(p) {
  # p = c(c, A, alpha, beta, gamma)
  t2_est <- stretched_surge_model(k_seq, p[1], p[2], p[3], p[4], p[5])
  
  # Calculate Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)
  err_t2 <- mean(abs((t2_val - t2_est) / t2_val))
  
  # Optional: Add a slight penalty to keep gamma within physically realistic bounds 
  # (though DEoptim bounds usually handle this, a soft penalty helps stability)
  penalty <- ifelse(p[5] <= 0, 1e6, 0)
  
  return(err_t2 + penalty)
}

# =========================================================================
# 3. Optimization Setup
# =========================================================================
set.seed(42)

# Bounds for p = c(c, A, alpha, beta, gamma)
# gamma (p[5]): allow anything from 0.1 (very stretched) to 3.0 (very compressed)
lower_bounds <- c(0.2, 0.001, 0.01, 0.001, 0.1)
upper_bounds <- c(0.8, 10,    5,    1,     3.0)

# Run Optimizer
fit_stretched <- DEoptim(obj_stretched, 
                         lower = lower_bounds, 
                         upper = upper_bounds, 
                         control = list(itermax = 1500, trace = FALSE))

# =========================================================================
# 4. Extract and Print Results
# =========================================================================
opt_p <- fit_stretched$optim$bestmem
cat("\n=== Stretched Surge Model Parameters ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Baseline (c): %.4f\n", opt_p[1]))
cat(sprintf("Scale (A): %.4f\n", opt_p[2]))
cat(sprintf("Power Rise (alpha): %.4f\n", opt_p[3]))
cat(sprintf("Exp Decay (beta): %.4f\n", opt_p[4]))
cat(sprintf("Shape Parameter (gamma): %.4f\n", opt_p[5]))

if (opt_p[5] > 1) {
  cat("Result: gamma > 1 (Compressed exponential: sharper drop-off)\n")
} else if (opt_p[5] < 1) {
  cat("Result: gamma < 1 (Stretched exponential: longer, dragging tail)\n")
}

# =========================================================================
# 5. Visualization
# =========================================================================
k_smooth <- exp(seq(log(min(k_seq)), log(max(k_seq)), length.out = 500))
t2_pred_smooth <- stretched_surge_model(k_smooth, opt_p[1], opt_p[2], opt_p[3], opt_p[4], opt_p[5])

df_pred <- data.frame(k = k_smooth, t2 = t2_pred_smooth)
df_orig <- data.frame(k = k_seq, t2 = t2_val) %>%
  mutate(Scale = ifelse(k >= 24, "Tail (>= 24h)", "Head (< 24h)"))

ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = df_pred, aes(x = k, y = t2), color = "#27ae60", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = df_orig, aes(x = k, y = t2, fill = Scale), size = 3, shape = 21, color="black") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Head (< 24h)" = "#f39c12", "Tail (>= 24h)" = "#2c3e50")) +
  labs(title = "Stretched Surge Model Fit (Kohlrausch)",
       subtitle = sprintf("gamma = %.3f | beta = %.4f", opt_p[5], opt_p[4]),
       x = "Temporal scale, k [h] (Log Scale)", 
       y = expression(t[2])) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
df<-master_regional_data$Final_Summary%>%filter(k_hours==1)
```
